User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the CineView application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a video available on CineView.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity.

url.description: URL of the video (e.g., YouTube iframe src).

Recommendation.title: Recommendation
Recommendation.description: Represents a video recommendation generated by the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Recommendation entity.

recommendedVideoId.description: Reference to Video that is recommended. (Relationship: Video 1:N Recommendation)

reason.description: Reason for the recommendation (e.g., 'Similar genre', 'User rating').

UserVideoInteraction.title: UserVideoInteraction
UserVideoInteraction.description: Represents a user's interaction with a video (e.g., watch history, rating).

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserVideoInteraction entity.

watchTimestamp.description: Timestamp of when the video was watched.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Only the authenticated user can access their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.  Corresponds to Firebase auth.uid.

definition.description: Stores video metadata. This is a public collection; no user-specific data here.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier of the video.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated video recommendations. Includes `videoId` and `recommendedVideoId` to link videos.

params.name: recommendationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the recommendation.

definition.description: Stores user-specific video interaction data (watch history, ratings). Only the authenticated user can access their own interactions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user. Corresponds to Firebase auth.uid.

params.name: interactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the video interaction.

definition.description: Store the current video the user is watching. Only accessible to the user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user. Corresponds to Firebase auth.uid.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support a video streaming platform with user-specific data and AI-powered recommendations. To address the 'Missing or insufficient permissions' error and ensure security, the structure enforces clear ownership and access control. The key is path-based ownership where the 'video' subcollection belongs to the user. This eliminates the need for complex rules involving `get()` calls. For example, access to `/users/{userId}/video/current` is governed by the `userId` in the path, making rules simple and robust. This design ensures Authorization Independence because access control is directly tied to the path and authentication, not based on data in other documents.

The structure segregates user-specific data from global video data. Each user has their own video and interaction data stored under their user ID (Structural Segregation). This allows secure `list` operations because listing is always scoped to a specific user's data. Recommendations are stored in a separate collection for AI processing and are linked to videos via `videoId` and `recommendedVideoId` fields. This design avoids mixing different access requirements in the same collection, and also allow secure `list` operations on the recommendation resource.

The `UserVideoInteraction` data maintains a history of user interactions, and these are also stored under the user's document, separated from other users' interactions. This separation ensures each user only has access to their own interaction data. 

This structure is scalable, debuggable, and ensures that security rules can be written in a straightforward manner based on the path and user authentication, without relying on complex data relationships.
